title: Identity, Permissions, Ownership, umask, and ACLs · Command Brief: Special Bits Acls
summary: Create a shared operations directory that grants required access without world-writable shortcuts or unexplained privilege escalation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux special bits acls increment in a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use setgid directories, sticky bit, setuid risk, and POSIX ACL masks only when the base ownership model is insufficient.
Derive default file and directory modes from requested modes and umask without treating umask as subtraction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Special Bits Acls
steps.instruction:
theory case — Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Special Bits Acls contract for the supplied data.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-special-bits-acls — Use setgid directories, sticky bit, setuid risk, and POSIX ACL masks only when the base ownership model is insufficient.
id
umask
stat -c 'mode=%a owner=%U group=%G path=%n' reports
# Derive directory create/delete behavior before any chmod or ACL change.
